supplier_name: Institute of Clinical Science and Technology (ICST)
one_line_value_proposition: Nationally deployed in 100% of Welsh GP practices — a population-scale COPD self-management app with 8 years of NHS partnership evidence and bilingual (English/Welsh) capability.
why_it_matters_locally: COPDhub integrates with annual review workflows to give patients a personalised digital care plan they can use year-round. Its 8-year NHS Wales deployment demonstrates population-scale adoption across all deprivation levels — making it a strong exemplar for commissioners considering equitable digital access.
target_problem_statement: Low patient activation between appointments, poor inhaler optimisation, lack of personalised self-management plans, and high carbon impact from inappropriate inhaler prescribing.
evidence_summary: Population-scale real-world evidence from 8-year NHS Wales national deployment (100% GP practice coverage). No standalone peer-reviewed RCT for COPDhub identified. Implementation science publication in Clinical Medicine (RCP) 2025 (Barry & Davies, PMC12395526). NICE HTE19 reviewed and recommended with conditions. ICST outcomes data shows 36% GP visit reduction and 19% A&E reduction among regular users.

case_studies.title: NHS Wales national deployment — population-level outcomes
case_studies.setting: 100% of NHS Wales GP practices and hospitals, 2019 to present
case_studies.outcome: 36% reduction in GP visits among regular users. 19% reduction in A&E admissions among regular users. 41% improvement in wellness scores at 4+ months. 20,303 tonnes CO₂ equivalent reduction from inhaler prescribing shift.
case_studies.caveat: Real-world data from national deployment — outcomes reflect users who engage regularly. Attribution to COPDhub specifically not verified by independent peer review. ICST-reported outcomes.

case_studies.title: Low-GWP inhaler prescribing shift — Wales
case_studies.outcome: Inhaler prescribing shifted from 30.8% to 41.1% DDD low-GWP — 20,303 tonnes CO₂ equivalent reduction. Most significant shift of any UK nation.
case_studies.caveat: Shift attributable to ICST toolkit broadly — not solely to COPDhub app. Reflects combined technology, clinical engagement and behaviour change work.

live_sites: NHS Wales (national — 100% GP practices), West Yorkshire ICB, NHS England expansion in progress, Hong Kong (Long COVID programme)
patients_covered_note: 20,000+ downloads in Wales. 99% of Welsh GP practices have patients using ICST respiratory app. NHS England expansion scope not publicly confirmed.
dtac_note: DTAC required for NHS commissioning. COPDhub-specific central DTAC pass not confirmed in reviewed public sources. Commissioners must request the DTAC form from ICST and complete local assessment.
gdpr_note: GDPR compliant. Specific data hosting location not confirmed in reviewed public sources.
target_patients: Adults aged 18+ with a COPD diagnosis (all severities). Available irrespective of age, disease severity, geographical location or socioeconomic status.
exclusions: iOS confirmed. Android — confirmation recommended before deployment. Web app not confirmed.
onboarding_detail: App freely available on App Store (NHS Wales branded). NHS-branded referral pathway from GP. ICST implementation facilitators support rollout in each region.
supplier_wrap: ICST provides implementation support, HCP education and change management alongside the technology. Educational modules embedded in platform. Continuous updates aligned with NICE/BTS/GOLD guidelines.
local_wraparound_detail: GP practice or community respiratory service must establish referral/invitation pathway. COPD register must be up to date. Co-production engagement with local clinical teams expected — allow time for this.
training_note: ICST provides HCP education and change management support.
monitoring_note: Guided self-help model. Clinician messaging confirmed. Full clinician dashboard specification not publicly confirmed — clarify with ICST before deployment.
escalation_note: Rescue plan feature activated by HCP. Patients must have clear routes to clinical support. Real-time deterioration guidance is built into the app but clinical escalation protocols must be defined locally.
operating_hours_caveat: COPDhub is a guided self-management tool, not a continuously monitored remote management service. Ensure local escalation pathways are clear.
implementation_prerequisites: Up-to-date COPD register for patient identification
GP practice or community service referral pathway established
indicative_price_text: Core COPDhub access is offered at no licence cost for eligible NHS organisations (as in NHS Wales national deployment). NHS England: optional implementation and analytics support is not included in the free offer and requires a separate commercial agreement — contact support@icst.org.uk.
service_wrap_description: No service wrap is included in the free offer. Local teams should assume self-service onboarding, internal adoption support, and local performance monitoring unless additional paid support is procured. ICST provides implementation science support and platform updates in the NHS Wales model — confirm England terms with ICST.
service_wrap_note: ICST provides implementation science support and ongoing platform updates as part of NHS Wales model. Free core in England typically excludes paid wrap — confirm with ICST.
contract_note: Direct from ICST. Free core access can be initiated with the supplier; paid support is contracted separately.
procurement_notes: Free core access can be initiated directly with the supplier. Any requested implementation support, account management, or enhanced reporting is contracted separately.
expected_benefit_note: NHS Wales population-scale signals include 36% GP visit reduction and 19% A&E reduction among regular users. May improve patient knowledge and self-management; should not be expected to deliver measurable admission reductions in isolation. Most valuable as a supplement to existing COPD pathways. NICE HTE19 evidence programme may refine cost-effectiveness.
tariff_considerations: No tariff implications as a commissioner-funded patient education tool under the free core model. Does not replace tariff-funded activity directly. Commissioners should still model using local COPD prevalence and activity costs where paid wrap is added.
provider_income_note: Minimal impact on provider income. May reduce time spent on certain educational components of COPD reviews but is unlikely to change overall review activity.
roi_note: No independently validated ROI model available. Supplier illustrative analysis suggests potential savings from reduced GP consultations, but this has not been tested in a controlled setting. 20,303 tonnes CO₂ equivalent reduction (Wales inhaler shift) supports Net Zero narratives. Treat cost-effectiveness claims as indicative; financial ROI pending NICE HTE19.
nhse_125k_note: Eligibility for NHSE ICB £125k COPD funding not confirmed for COPDhub. Verify with NHSE before including in business case.
demo_notes: Contact ICST at support@icst.org.uk to request a demonstration.
source_summary: NICE HTG736, ICST outcomes page (icst.org.uk/outcomes), Barry & Davies Clinical Medicine (RCP) 2025 (PMC12395526), NHS Wales App Store listing, RCP Digital Health report.
confidence_note: Content based on publicly available information. COPDhub-specific peer-reviewed RCT not identified. Verify all claims with ICST before use in procurement documentation.
sustainability_highlight: 20,303 tonnes CO₂ equivalent reduction from inhaler prescribing shift in Wales (2018–2024). Relevant for ICB Net Zero commitments.

clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: 100% of NHS Wales GP practices and hospitals, 2019 to present
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 36% reduction in GP visits among regular users. 19% reduction in A&E admissions among regular users. 41% improvement in wellness scores at 4+ months use.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: ICST-reported outcomes. Not independently peer-reviewed. Regular users only — outcomes do not represent the full deployed population.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Low-GWP inhaler prescribing shift — NHS Wales (2018–2024)
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Low-GWP inhaler prescribing shifted from 30.8% to 41.1% DDD (2018–2024). 20,303 tonnes CO₂ equivalent reduction. Most significant prescribing shift of any UK nation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Attributable to the ICST toolkit broadly — not to COPDhub alone. Reflects combined technology, clinical engagement and behaviour change work.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Documents key enablers of 100% national adoption: co-production, digital infrastructure, clinical leadership, embedding into workflows, audit/QI tools. Open access.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Implementation science publication — describes implementation methodology rather than clinical outcomes RCT.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: No standalone peer-reviewed RCT for COPDhub has been identified in publicly reviewed sources. The primary evidence base is population-scale real-world data from NHS Wales over 8 years of national deployment. Full evidence submitted to NICE HTE19 is held by NICE.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Absence of RCT is noted. NICE HTE19 reviewed and recommended with conditions.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: No peer-reviewed RCT identified. Evidence base is population-scale real-world data. NICE HTE19 evidence generation programme will address this.

monitoring_model: Self-management with clinician dashboard for population-level oversight.
minimum_conditions_for_success: GP practice engagement, respiratory pathway lead, integration into prescribing review workflow.
procurement_notes: Available via direct contract with ICST. Confirm G-Cloud availability.
cyber_notes: Confirm current security certifications with ICST.

context_of_use.population: Adults with COPD across all severity levels
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Self-management education, inhaler technique training, prescribing decision support
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Minimal — clinician dashboard available for population oversight
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTE19 (HTG736) — recommended during 3-year evidence generation period
